content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom indices labeled. Looking at atom O:14 (the red sphere in the lower right), I can clearly see it's directly bonded to:
- S:13 (yellow/gold sulfur atom) - connected by a yellow rod
- H:39 (white hydrogen atom) - connected by a white rod

Let me confirm if there are any other neighbors by rotating the molecule slightly to get a better perspective of atom 14.

From the current image, I can see that O:14 has exactly two covalent bonds: one to S:13 (sulfur) and one to H:39 (hydrogen). This makes sense chemically as oxygen typically forms two bonds in neutral molecules.

The geomconf_index for this molecule appears to be 8222, based on the file name "geomconf_8222.xyz".

So the neighbor atoms of atom 14 (O) are:
- Atom index 13, element S (sulfur)
- Atom index 39, element H (hydrogen)

And the geomconf_index is 8222.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 14, whose element should be O. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
